articles.title: RB Choudary Final Rites: Details of Time, Venue, and Arrangements Of Funeral
articles.description: RB Choudary Final Rites: Veteran producer RB Choudary died in a road accident near Udaipur while travelling for a family wedding. His final rites will be held in Chennai on May 6.

articles.title: Security alert in Punjab after Jalandhar and Amritsar blast incidents
articles.description: In Jalandhar, a parked scooter exploded near the Border Security Force (BSF) Headquarters in a high-security zone late last night. The area was immediately cordoned off and police launched a detailed investigation. Sources suggest that a National Investigation Agency (NIA) team is en route to assist in the probe. In another incident, a blast was reported around 10:50 PM in the Khassa cantonment area of Amritsar. Morning visuals from the site show heightened security as investigations continue inside the cantonment zone.

articles.title: 'Vijay stays rooted despite his stardom', says Dil Raju, Vamshi Paidipally calls him 'man of strong conviction' after Tamil Nadu Assembly election win
articles.description: Producer Dil Raju and director Vamshi Paidipally have praised Vijay’s grounded nature and strong conviction following his political success. Reflecting on their experience working with him, they highlighted his desire to serve people and his ability to stay humble despite immense stardom.

articles.title: California entrepreneur loses 29 kilos, shares 25 rules that help him
articles.description: An overweight individual's practical 25-rule guide to achieving 13% body fat is gaining traction. His approach emphasizes simple, real-world strategies like prioritizing protein, consistent strength training, and mindful eating over quick fixes. This journey offers hope and direction for millions struggling with obesity, highlighting that consistency and smart habits are key to sustainable weight loss.

articles.title: Every 6th BJP MP in Lok Sabha won through 'vote chori': Rahul Gandhi
articles.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi on Wednesday alleged that every sixth BJP MP in the Lok Sabha secured his or her seat through "vote chori" and asked if they should be labelled "ghuspethiya" in that party's own parlance. Gandhi also claimed that the BJP would not be able to win even 140 seats today in the Lok Sabha if fair elections are held. "Through vote theft, sometimes individual seats are stolen, and at other times, an entire government. Of the 240 BJP MPs in the Lok Sabha, roughly every sixth MP secured seat through vote chori," the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha said in a post in Hindi on X. "They are not hard to identify - should we, in the BJP's own parlance, label them 'ghuspethiya'? And what of Haryana? There, the entire government itself is an 'ghuspethiya'. The very institutions they keep in their pockets - the ones they manipulate to distort voter lists and the electoral process - are themselves 'remote-controlled'," Gandhi said, in a scathing attack on the ...

articles.title: In Amritsar blast near BSF-Army zone sparks panic; two-wheeler near boundary wall suspected to be source
articles.description: Late at night in Amritsar's Khasa area, a loud explosion emanating from a stationary two-wheeler rattled the atmosphere near crucial military sites belonging to the BSF and Army. Fortunately, no one was hurt; however, the explosion did cause significant damage to the outer walls of nearby establishments.

articles.title: IMD forecast warns of widespread rain and storms in these states: How India’s changing weather may impact travellers
articles.description: India's weather is set for unpredictable conditions over the next two weeks, with thunderstorms, showers, and localized heatwaves impacting travel. Northern regions face isolated showers and strong winds, while East and Northeast India anticipate heavy rains. Southern states will experience continuous rainfall, and sporadic heatwave conditions are possible in Rajasthan.

articles.title: Sun Pharma explores funding mix for $12 billion Organon deal
articles.description: Global lenders are exploring various financing options for Sun Pharmaceutical Industries' proposed $12 billion acquisition of Organon & Co. The Indian drugmaker is considering swapping Organon bondholders into Sun Pharma debt and a potential euro-denominated bond. Additionally, Sun Pharma plans to raise $3 billion to $4 billion in offshore loans, with deal approvals anticipated by December.

articles.title: ‘Not censorship’: India responds sharply to NYT op-ed
articles.description: India’s Ambassador Vinay Mohan Kwatra rejects NYT op-ed claims of censorship, saying IT rules focus on platform accountability and misinformation control.

articles.title: High TPC levels! Dark, degraded oil found in popular fast food chain: Study explains its impact on your health
articles.description: A recent food safety scare has put the spotlight on reused cooking oil risks after a KFC outlet in Kondapur, Hyderabad was reportedly found using degraded oil with unsafe Total Polar Compounds (TPC) levels during an inspection. Repeated heating of oil increases TPC, making it harmful, with the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India setting a 25% safety limit. High TPC levels are linked to serious health risks like hypertension, heart disease, and liver damage, raising concerns over food safety practices in fast food chains.

This ability to express oneself is the first step towards personal growth and societal change.
